title: Cacop

The '''Cacops''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the celestial body [[Veccacopia]]<ref name="NEGTC" /> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Tunka sector/Legends|Tunka sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Veccacopia system/Legends|Veccacopia system]]</ref> They had [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] with retinae, and the visual appearance of individual Cacops tended to be difficult to distinguish for members of other species.<ref name="NEGTC" />

Veccacopia was discovered by the wider [[galactic community]]<ref name="NEGTC" /> at some point before [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]]<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' states that [[Veccacopia]] had been discovered at some point prior to [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] becoming a [[Senator/Legends|senator]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes Valorum was a senator by [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], it can be deduced that the celestial body had been discovered by that date.</ref> and was designated a [[Republic protectorate|protectorate]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], Veccacopia's judicial office grew corrupt, with convicted Cacops hiring Cacop stand-ins to serve their sentences instead.<ref name="NEGTC" />

Not long after Veccacopia's discovery, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] served as a [[Judge/Legends|judge]] there. When Valorum learned of the Cacops' deception and the inability of his [[Bailiff/Legends|bailiffs]] to distinguish between the guilty individuals and the doubles, he broke the corruption ring of the local judicial office by instituting a mandatory [[Retinal scan/Legends|retinal scan]].<ref name="NEGTC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Cacops were introduced in the [[2002]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="NEGTC" />
